name: Filipino Cuisine Tips, 1 of 2
recipeInstructions:
1. E-mail dialogue between Manny Rothstein, Dennis Santiago, and the original sender discusses various Filipino recipes. The recipient finds them a great blend of exotic and easy-to-prepare dishes.
2. Most people avoid pigs blood but highly recommend trying it if you enjoy more exotic cuisines.
3. Bagoong: A salty shrimp mixture sold in 1/2 pint jars in the refrigerated foods section of oriental stores or available as an import from the Philippines. No substitutes are known.
4. Patis: Filipino fish sauce, identical to Vietnamese fish sauce, made from fish ingredients. Salt can be used if unavailable.
5. Traditional side dishes:
a. Rice is always served with Filipino meals, acting as a healthy base and flavor enhancer for meat dishes.
b. A simple side dish includes sliced or diced tomatoes (2-3), chopped onion (1/2), cilantro for flavor and color, and patis (3-4 tablespoons) mixed in a bowl to serve alongside rice and the main dish.
6. Filipino meals often feature multiple main dishes served together, similar to Chinese cuisine where various courses are offered.
(continued in part 2)
